arts.ttl: Hull City must stand up to the Luton Town fight in bid to win the war
arts.sum: Their form at Kenilworth Road is the one relative shining light of an otherwise miserable start - three wins and defeats with two draws is their record so far, but their 5-1 thumping at Middlesbrough before the break will go one of two ways when Saturday's game gets going. Luton Town may have endured a nightmare start to the Championship season, and boss Rob Edwards may be fighting to save his job, but Hull City can still expect one of their toughest assignments yet on Saturday afternoon.

arts.ttl: Spirited Edwards is more determined than ever to turn things around at Luton
arts.sum: Boss eager to be a success with the Hatters
International football is consigned to the memory bank for 2024 and there is a full slate of Championship action this weekend as Phil Agius previews all 12 games.

arts.sum: Hull City boss Tim Walter has said Brighton and Hove Albion loan goalkeeper Carl Rushworth is ‘getting better… The post Hull City boss shares latest injury update on Brighton loan man Carl Rushworth appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Luton defender focused on matters in hand despite uncertainty surrounding Hatters boss
arts.sum: McGuinness knows things haven’t been good enough at Kenilworth Road

arts.ttl: Abu Kamara to return in Hull City's predicted starting XI for Luton Town meeting
arts.sum: Kamara is available again following a two-game lay-off after picking up an ankle injury against Portsmouth at the beginning of November, and his return is a timely boost for Walter given he's without Liam Millar and Mohamed Belloumi for the rest of the season with ACL injuries. The Tigers boss started with Mason Burstow out on the right of his attacking unit against West Brom last time out and Abdus Omur on the left, but Kamara may well return and could operate on his favoured right side.

arts.ttl: 'Bad loser' Tim Walter is determined to repay Acun Ilicali's Hull City faith
arts.sum: Surrounded by his wife and children, Walter took a rare opportunity to recharge his batteries after a difficult period which saw his side go without a win in the six games since the October break saw City head off to Antalya for a warm weather training camp, and not yield the immediate results they hoped it would. Amid growing speculation around his job at Hull City, Tim Walter spent the first week of the international break trying to help his family settle into life in England, and East Yorkshire.

arts.ttl: Preview: Luton Town vs. Hull City - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Championship clash between Luton Town and Hull City, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Three games to shape Tim Walter's Hull City future as Tigers face pivotal week
arts.sum: A little under two weeks ago, when Luton Town were thrashed 5-1 at Middlesbrough - their eighth defeat of this fledging season - and Hull City succumbed to yet another dispiriting home loss, there was an expectation in football that both managers would be on holiday come 3pm this weekend, while another manager, be that caretaker or permanent, took charge of their respective teams. For Luton, relegation from the Premier League was naturally going to have an effect, but few could have predicted the Hatters would lose eight of their first 15 games and be hovering above the drop zone by only goal difference, but Edwards deserves the time to turn things around, given he took them to the top flight in the first place.
